corpus.name: A1/C1-escape-by-return
corpus.code: fn leak() -> Entry<'?> { let t = table::new(); let e = t.entry(k); return e; }
corpus.why: R13: e's region is t's local block region, not a region parameter of leak; a token can never outlive its source's frame.

corpus.name: A2/C2-token-in-struct-or-heap
corpus.code: struct Holder { e: Entry<'h> }  // and: let bx = box_new(own e)
corpus.why: R1 rejects the non-confined declaration with a confined field (generic wrappers caught post-monomorphization); R2 rejects the heap store statement.

corpus.name: A3/C3-two-unique-tokens
corpus.code: let e1 = t.entry(k1); let e2 = t.entry(k2);
corpus.why: R5: a uniq issue requires no entry overlapping t; (t,uniq,e1) is live. Same rule kills get-two-and-swap; the blessed route is a sealed two-key op.

corpus.name: A4/C5-two-shared-cursors
corpus.code: let c1 = t.range(lo,mid); let c2 = t.range(mid,hi); let v = t.get(k); let n1 = c1.next(); let n2 = c2.next();
corpus.why: R5/R6 shr regime: shr issues and &-receiver ops coexist; both cursors auto-consume at scope end (R7).

corpus.name: A5/C4-shared-then-unique-upgrade
corpus.code: let c = t.range(lo,hi); let e = t.entry(k);
corpus.why: R5: uniq issue while a shr entry lives on t; no upgrade path exists — consume the cursor first.

corpus.name: A6/C6-move-while-loaned
corpus.code: let c = t.range(lo,hi); let t2 = own t;
corpus.why: R6: a place overlapped by a live entry cannot be moved, own-passed, or dropped.

corpus.name: A7/C7-arm-imbalanced-consume
corpus.code: let g = m.lock(); match flag { T => { unlock_via(&m, own g); } F => { noop(); } }
corpus.why: R12: fall-through arms disagree on loans(m) and g's liveness; blessed shape consumes in both arms or in neither.

corpus.name: A8/C8-helper-issue-and-reuse
corpus.code:
fn reserve<'t>(t: &uniq 't table<K,V>, k: K) -> Entry<'t> { let e = t.entry(k); return e; }
let e = reserve(&uniq tt, k); e.fill(v); tt.insert(k2, v2);
corpus.why: R9 tie ('t -> t) is well-formed; R10(b) derives the uniq issue at the call from the signature alone; the return-effect check (R10c) matches; fill consumes, insert then legal — the wrapped-writer blessed idiom.

corpus.name: A9-preloop-token-consumed-in-body
corpus.code: let e = t.entry(k); loop { e.fill(v); match done() { T => { break; } F => { noop(); } } }
corpus.why: R12: the reachable back edge's projected state (e dead, loans empty) differs from loop entry (e live, uniq entry on t).

corpus.name: A10-per-iteration-token
corpus.code: loop { let k = next_k(); let e = t.entry(k); e.fill(v); match done() { T => { break; } F => { noop(); } } } t.insert(k9, v9);
corpus.why: R12's projection excludes body-locals after body scope-end processing, so back edge equals entry; issue/consume balance per iteration.

corpus.name: A11-cross-source-reissue (hard call)
corpus.code:
fn transfer<'a,'b>(x: &'a ring<T>, y: &'b ring<T>, p: own Prod<'a>) -> Prod<'b> { retire(&x, own p); let q = y.producer(); return q; }
let pa = a.producer(); let pb = transfer(&a, &b, own pa); let r = a.endpoint_reset();
corpus.why: Honest composition, not laundering: R10(c) seeds (x,shr,p) in the body; retire consumes it; y.producer() freshly issues; return at caller-supplied 'b passes R13 and the exit-effect check; caller books consume-on-a plus issue-on-b, both place- and kind-exact.

corpus.name: A12-ambiguous-region-signature
corpus.code: fn pick<'t>(x: &'t table<K,V>, y: &'t table<K,V>, sel: B, ...) -> Cursor<'t> { ... }
corpus.why: R9: result region 't ties to two borrow-mode parameters; rejected at declaration so source places stay singleton-exact (reject-when-unsure).

corpus.name: C9-loop-carried-cursor-reads
corpus.code: let c = t.range(lo,hi); loop { let n = c.next(); match n { Some(x) => { let v = t.get(x); } None => { break } } } let w = t.get(k2);
corpus.why: R6 shr regime permits &-receiver get under the live shr entry; back edge equals entry (R12); read-under-cursor typechecks.

corpus.name: C10-loop-reissue-without-rebind
corpus.code: let g = m.lock(); loop { match predicate(&g) { Done => { break } Wait => { let g2 = cv.wait(&m, own g); } } }
corpus.why: R12: Wait arm ends with g dead and holder g2 (auto-consumed at arm end, unlocking); projected back-edge/arm states disagree with entry (g live); iteration 2 would run unlocked on dead g.

corpus.name: C11-early-consume-then-reopen
corpus.code: let e = t.entry(k); e.fill(v); let e2 = t.entry(k2); e2.fill(v2); { let e3 = t.entry(k3); } let c = t.range(lo,hi); let x = t.get(k4);
corpus.why: R7 removes entries at explicit consume and at inner-scope auto-consume (e3's drop releases); sequential uniq sessions then a shr session compose.

corpus.name: C12-wrap-then-helper-consume (hard call, amended)
corpus.why: R8 renames the entry's holder e->w at construction; R9 totality forces threading t (the original finish(w)-only signature rejects at declaration, zero ties); the call consumes under R6's sole-loan exception, and the callee is seeded (t,uniq,w) so it cannot mutate t before consuming w.

corpus.name: C13-relock-brand-confusion
corpus.code:
fn relock<'r>(m: &'r mutex<T>, g: own Guard<'r>) -> Guard<'r> { ... }
let g1 = m1.lock(); let gx = relock(&m2, own g1);
corpus.why: R10(a): g1's recorded source place m1 differs from the tied argument place m2; place identity is the brand and cannot be laundered.

corpus.name: C14-uniq-token-into-replicate-slot
corpus.code: let g = m.lock(); par::for_chunks(&uniq data, &g, body);
corpus.why: R14: a holder holding any uniq entry cannot pass a replicate-shared slot; duplicating unique interior access across bodies would be a race by construction.

corpus.name: ATK-gut-untied-param
corpus.code: fn gut<'t,'u>(t: &uniq 't table<K,V>, e: own Entry<'u>) { t.insert(k2,v2); e.fill(v); }
corpus.why: R9: 'u ties to zero borrow-mode parameters — undeclarable. The single-region rewrite gut<'t>(t, e: own Entry<'t>) declares, but R10(c) seeds (t,uniq,e) so the insert-before-fill body order rejects at R6; only fill-then-insert (safe) checks.

corpus.name: ATK-launder-strength
corpus.code:
fn launder<'t>(t: &'t table<K,V>, e: own Entry<'t>) -> Entry<'t> { return e; }
let e2 = launder(&t, own e); let c = t.range(lo,hi); let n1 = c.next(); e2.fill(v1);
corpus.why: Kind travels with the confined type (R1/R10b), never with the tie's mode: e2 is reissued as a uniq entry, so t.range's shr issue rejects at R5; no &-tied slot can downgrade a uniq token to shr.

corpus.name: ATK-probe-callee-blind
corpus.code: fn probe<'t>(t: &'t table<K,V>, e: own Entry<'t>) { let c = t.range(lo,hi); let n = c.next(); e.fill(v); let n2 = c.next(); }
corpus.why: R10(c) seeds (t,uniq,e) at body entry; the shr issue t.range rejects at R5 — the callee frame can no longer forget the caller's loan.

corpus.name: ATK-refresh-reissue-laundering
corpus.code: fn refresh<'t>(t: &uniq 't table<K,V>, e: own Entry<'t>) -> Entry<'t> { t.insert(k9,v9); return e; }
corpus.why: Under the R10(c) seed, R6 rejects the insert (uniq entry live, statement does not consume e); a body of just { return e } is legal but harmless — an identity reissue over an unmutated source.

corpus.name: ATK-relocate-two-uniq
corpus.code: fn relocate<'t>(t: &uniq 't table<K,V>, e: own Entry<'t>) -> Entry<'t> { let e2 = t.entry(k2); return e2; }
corpus.why: R5 under the seed: a uniq issue while (t,uniq,e) is live; the front-door route to two live uniq tokens is closed.

corpus.name: ATK-close-one-sibling
corpus.code: let c1 = tt.range(a,b); let c2 = tt.range(c,d); close_one(&uniq tt, own c1); let n = c2.next();
corpus.why: R6's consume-exception requires the consumed holder's entry to be the SOLE entry on tt; (tt,shr,c2) is live, so the &uniq mint rejects and c2 cannot be dangled.

corpus.name: ATK-delete-at-sole-cursor (hard call)
corpus.code:
let c = t.range(lo,hi);  // only token
t.delete_at(own c);      // delete_at(&uniq 't btree, own Cursor<'t>): consumes
corpus.why: R6 sole-loan exception: the statement surrenders the only token while mutating, atomic at statement granularity — no sibling exists to dangle; this recovers the SQLite delete-at-cursor shape.

corpus.name: ATK-maybetok-none-forgery
corpus.code:
confined(uniq) enum MaybeTok<'r> { Some(Entry<'r>), None }
let none = MaybeTok::None<'r_tt>; let e2 = open(&uniq tt, own none);
corpus.why: R5: a user construction leaving any region parameter unbound by a captured field is rejected, so no loanless forged-region token exists; R10(a)'s must-hold-the-loan check would independently reject the call.

corpus.name: ATK-wview-kind-incoherence
corpus.code: confined(shr) struct WView<'r> { p: &uniq 'r slot }
corpus.why: R1 coherence: a confined(shr) type transitively containing a &uniq field is ill-formed, so shared tokens can never carry unique interior write paths (protects OWN-5 and the optimizer's shr-window fact).

corpus.name: ATK-cloak-kind
corpus.code: confined(shr) struct Cloak<'r> { inner: Entry<'r> }  // Entry is uniq-kind
corpus.why: R1 coherence: shr wrapper containing a confined(uniq) field is ill-formed; kind can never be re-keyed by wrapping (R8 transfers never change kind).

corpus.name: ATK-db-struct-field-place
corpus.code:
struct Db { t: table<K,V>, u: table<K,V> }
let e = db.t.entry(k); db.t.insert(k2,v2); e.fill(v);
corpus.why: Loans are place-keyed (R4): entry (db.t,uniq,e) freezes db.t and db (overlap) but not db.u; the insert rejects at R6 exactly like P2 — struct-held forms are fully supported, with fill-then-insert accepting.

corpus.name: ATK-two-struct-rings
corpus.code:
struct Two { a: ring<T>, b: ring<T> }
let pt = two.a.producer(); feed(&two.b, &uniq pt, v);
corpus.why: R10(a): pt's source place two.a differs from the tied argument place two.b; brands are resolved places, so one level of struct wrapping cannot bypass the P5 verdict.

corpus.name: ATK-drop-order-rebind
corpus.code: let t1 = table::new(); let e1 = t1.entry(k1); e1.fill(v1); let t2 = table::new(); let e2 = t2.entry(k2); rebind e1 = e2;  // scope end follows
corpus.why: R7 checks the derived drop itself: at scope end t2 is reached while (t2,uniq,e1) is live — a checked error; safety no longer rests on binding-order position, which the rebind falsified.

corpus.name: ATK-destructure-env-struct
corpus.code: match env { Env { t: tb } => { let tbl2 = own tbl; let v = tb.get(k); } }
corpus.why: R11: struct patterns never destructure confined values (only whole-token enum destructure exists), and R3 keeps field access statement-scoped, so no bare borrow binding can outlive the entry that guards it.

corpus.name: ATK-entryresult-occ-vac
corpus.code:
confined(uniq) enum EntryResult<'t> { Occ(OccTok<'t>), Vac(VacTok<'t>) }
let r = t.entry(k); match r { Occ(o) => { o.replace(v); } Vac(v0) => { v0.fill(v); } }
corpus.why: R11: every variant packs its payload into exactly one same-kind, same-region token; the scrutinee's entry renames per arm and both arms consume, so R12 joins agree — the flagship hashbrown occupied/vacant API is writable.

corpus.name: ATK-trylock-enum
corpus.code:
confined(uniq) enum LockResult<'m> { Got(Guard<'m>), Busy }
let r = m.try_lock(); match r { Got(g) => {...} Busy => {} }
corpus.why: R11: the Busy variant carries zero token fields while the scrutinee holds an entry (issue is unconditional at the call); the blessed shape is the state-queryable token (next entry).

corpus.name: ATK-acquire-helper (hard call)
corpus.why: Retry-acquire crosses the loop by RETURN, not break: F arm consumes so the back edge equals entry (R12); the T-arm return passes R13 and R10(c)'s exit-effect check (entries on m = exactly the signature-implied issue held by the result); the caller derives a fresh uniq issue.

corpus.name: self-tie-env-param
corpus.code: fn body(chunk: &uniq 'c chunkview<i64>, env: &'e Env<'e>) reads('e) writes('c) { ... }
corpus.why: R9 self-tie: no distinct borrow parameter carries 'e and env is by-borrow, so the signature is well-formed; self-ties seed nothing and skip the call-site source comparison (and cannot be re-passed into a distinct-tie slot — reject-when-unsure).

corpus.name: ATK-next-view-sibling-issue
corpus.code:
let c = t.range(lo,hi); loop { let v = c.next_view();  // form clause: issues View on source(receiver), shr
  ...view dies at body end each iteration... }
corpus.why: R5 holder-receiver issue: the loan lands on c's recorded source place as a sibling shr entry (t,shr,v); zero-copy scan views work without per-element re-lookup, preserving the B-tree/SQLite performance shape.

corpus.name: ATK-mk-env2-multi-source
corpus.code: fn mk_env<'r1,'r2>(t1: &'r1 table<K,V>, t2: &'r2 table<K,V>) -> Env2<'r1,'r2> { let env = Env2 { lut: &t1, dict: &t2 }; return env; }
corpus.why: R9: each region ties to exactly one parameter; R10(b) derives one shr issue per tied argument place — multi-borrow env helpers compose, fixing the exactly-one-issuer wrong-rejection.

corpus.name: ATK-let-move-holder
corpus.code: let g = m.lock(); let g2 = move g;
corpus.why: R8: a plain let-move renames the entry's holder to g2 (never drops or duplicates it); the unlock runs at g2's scope end, so the lost-release completion cannot be implemented.

corpus.name: ATK-loop-all-paths-exit
corpus.code: let e = t.entry(k); loop { e.fill(v); break; }
corpus.why: R12: every body path ends in break/return, so the back edge is syntactically unreachable and only the (single) break state is checked — equivalent to the straight-line program.

corpus.name: ATK-memoizing-shared-get
corpus.code:
// form table: table.get declared & receiver, internals memoize the last lookup
par::for_chunks(&uniq data, &env, body);  // bodies call env.lut.get(k) concurrently
corpus.why: R15: an & receiver op on a loanable form must preserve interior addresses AND be safe under concurrent invocation; the memoizing declaration fails hostile fact-channel review, so no program lawfully links against it.

corpus.name: ATK-zero-region-endpoint
corpus.code:
confined(uniq) struct Tok { }  // no region parameters
fn escape() -> Tok { let q = conc_queue::new(); let p = q.producer(); return p; }
corpus.why: R1: every loanable confined type must carry at least one region parameter; with it, R13 rejects the return (local region), so the queue can never die under a live endpoint.

decidability: The checker carries, per function, one environment: a binding map (name -> type, owned/reference, live/consumed, declaration index; for confined bindings also the type's declared kind and a region-parameter -> source-place map) and one loan table, a finite set of (resolved place, kind, holder) entries where a resolved place is a root binding plus a field path. A declarations pre-pass validates confined-type formation (R1) and resolves every signature's region ties (R9) by counting borrow-mode parameters per region — a rejection, not a search, when the count is not exactly one. The body pass then visits each ANF statement exactly once in program order, dispatching on syntactic form; a call's loan effects are a positional lookup against the callee's declared form clause or pre-resolved ties (no unification search at the call site), and every legality condition (R5 preconditions, R6 freeze, R10a place-brand equality) is an emptiness/membership test over the loan table using path-prefix overlap. Effects apply in a fixed order: legality, consumes, issues. Match arms are each checked once from a copy of the entry state and their projected exit states (entries and liveness of outside-declared bindings, taken after arm-local scope-end processing) compared for equality; loops check the body once and compare the projected back-edge state to the entry state — equality replaces any fixpoint, because a body that maps the state to itself does so on every iteration and any other body is rejected — with back-edge reachability decided syntactically (every path ends in break/return). Breaks and returns contribute states to their exit joins under the same pairwise equality. Total cost is O(statements x loan-table size x maximum path length); there are no dataflow lattices, no path sensitivity, no fixpoints, and no backtracking, because every ambiguity (untied or doubly tied regions, unbound construction regions, divergent joins, place mismatches) rejects instead of branching.

derivations.derivation: S1: result not confined, no issue; loan table {}. S2: push declared (clause none) [R15]; statement-scoped &uniq mint on place s is legal because no entry overlaps s [R6]; mint consumed by the call (T-A); no issue. S3: identical — minting is per statement, table still {}. Scope end: no live holders; s drops clean [R7]. The loan judgment is vacuous on unloaned bindings, so statement-scoped minting composes unchanged.

S1: table {}. S2: uniq issue on place t requires no overlapping entry — holds [R5]; table {(t,uniq,e)}, src(e)={'t: t}. S3: the statement mints &uniq t while a uniq entry lives and neither consumes nor reissues its holder e — REJECT [R6] (the insert could rehash and dangle e's interior pointer). Had S3 and S4 been swapped, fill would first empty the table [R7] and the insert would then be legal.

S2: shr issue on t (no uniq entry overlaps) [R5]; table {(t,shr,c)}. Loop entry: {(t,shr,c)}, c live. S3: statement-scoped &uniq mint on holder c [R3]; no entry overlaps place c, clause none — state unchanged. Some arm: remove mints &uniq t while shr entries live and the statement consumes nothing — REJECT [R6 shr regime] (remove could delete or move the node under the cursor). The None arm's break at loop-entry state would otherwise have been the legal exit.

S1: uniq issue on m [R5]; table {(m,uniq,g)}, src(g)={'m: m}. Loop entry E = {(m,uniq,g)}, g live. S2: & mint from holder g [R3]; self-tied signature imposes no source comparison and no effect. Done arm: break at E. Wait arm: the statement consumes g whose entry is the SOLE entry on m, so the declared & mint of m is permitted despite the uniq freeze [R6 exception]; R10(a): g's source place m equals the tied argument place m; consume then issue [R7 then R5]: {(m,uniq,g2)}; rebind g = g2 renames the holder [R8]: {(m,uniq,g)}, g live — arm exit = E. Join: both arms at E [R12]; back edge = E = entry. Scope end: reverse order — g auto-consumed (release = unlock), table {} [R7]; cv, m are reference params and expire.

S3: shr issue [R5]; {(a,shr,pa)}, src(pa)={'r: a}. S4: {(b,shr,cb)}. S5: R10(a): the confined argument pa's tied region 'r is bound to argument place b, but pa's live entry records source place a; a and b are distinct resolved places — REJECT [R10(a)]. Region equality cannot rescue it (both rings share the block region): the brand is the resolved place. The symmetric swap fails identically, and R15's fail-closed table means no op accepts a token without a tie to compare.

S1: the construction instantiates 'e from the captured & mint of tbl (no unbound region) [R5]; kind shr is coherent (only a & field) [R1]; issue: {(tbl,shr,env)}, src(env)={'e: tbl}. body's signature: 'c ties to chunk, 'e self-ties to env [R9]. S2: split-unique slot mints &uniq data — no entry overlaps data [R6/R14]; replicate-shared slot mints & of holder env — all of env's held entries are shr, so replication is permitted [R14]; body rows match slot modes; all mints are consumed by the par statement, which joins every body before returning, and 'a plus the block region enclose the join. State after: {(tbl,shr,env)} — any &uniq/own op on tbl during env's life rejects [R6]. S3: no entry overlaps data — legal. Scope end: env auto-consumed [R7], table {}; params expire.

machine_spec:
CHECKER STATE (per function). (1) Bindings: map name -> {type; mode in {owned, reference(region)}; status in {live, consumed}; decl_index; if confined: kind(type) in {uniq, shr} read off the declaration, and src: map from each region parameter of the type to a resolved place}. (2) LoanTable: set of entries (place P, kind k, holder h). A resolved place is (root binding, field path — possibly empty); overlap(P,Q) iff same root and one path is a prefix of the other. (3) Block stack with per-block declared-binding lists. (4) During matches/loops: saved snapshots (deep copies of 1+2) and collected break/return states. Nothing else is mutable state.

DECLARATIONS PRE-PASS (runs before any body; all failures reject the declaration). (a) Confined types: only confined types may contain borrow or confined fields; every region used by a field appears in the parameter list; loanable confined types have >=1 region parameter; kind coherence — a confined(shr) type must not transitively contain a &uniq field or a confined(uniq) field. Non-confined types containing either are ill-formed; after monomorphization, re-check every instantiated type (catches generic wrappers and sealed-form type arguments — instantiating any form/generic type parameter with a confined type rejects). (b) Signature ties: for each region parameter 'p of each confined parameter or confined result: let D = distinct borrow-mode parameters (not the confined parameter itself) whose declared region is 'p. If |D| = 1: tie('p) = that parameter. If |D| = 0 and the confined parameter is itself passed by borrow: tie('p) = SELF. Otherwise reject. Record the derived effect: consumes = the own-mode confined parameters; issues = for each confined result region 'p, (tie('p), kind(result type)). (c) Form table: every op of a loanable form must carry exactly one clause — issues K, issues K on source(receiver), consumes, reissues, none; any call not covered by a clause or a derived signature rejects (fail-closed). & receiver ops on loanable forms carry the address-stability + concurrent-safety obligation (review gate, outside the checker).

STATEMENT TRANSITIONS (one pass, ANF order; each statement: resolve operand places -> legality -> consumes -> issues -> liveness updates).

[Mint legality, used everywhere] Minting & of place Q: reject if any uniq entry overlaps Q, unless EXC. Minting &uniq of Q, or moving/own-passing/assigning/explicitly-dropping Q: reject if ANY entry overlaps Q, unless EXC. EXC (consume-exception): this statement consumes or reissues a holder h, h's entries are the ONLY entries overlapping Q, and the minted borrow of Q is declared by the callee's signature. A live confined binding mints at most one statement-scoped borrow per statement; field projection through a live confined binding or a borrow of one yields only a statement-scoped borrow consumed by the receiving call; both transfer no entries.

[let x = f(args) / op call] (i) For each confined argument h (own or borrowed), for each region 'p of its type with tie('p) != SELF: let R = the resolved place of the argument bound to tie('p); require status(h)=live, src(h)['p] = R, and (R, kind(h), h) in LoanTable; else reject. SELF ties: skip the comparison; additionally, passing a self-tied-origin confined value into a distinct-tie slot rejects (its src is unknown — reject-when-unsure). (ii) Mint legality for every borrow argument per above. (iii) Consumes: for each own confined argument consumed by the clause/derived effect: remove all entries with that holder; mark it consumed. Other own arguments: require no overlapping entries; mark consumed. (iv) Issues: if the result type is confined, for each region 'p of it: source place S = place of the argument at tie('p); for clause `issues on source(receiver)`, S = src(receiver-holder)[the type's region]. Precondition (checked AFTER consumes): kind uniq -> no entry overlaps S; kind shr -> no uniq entry overlaps S; else reject. Add (S, kind(result type), x); set src(x)['p] = S. The result must bind a fresh let or a rebind. Region for escape accounting: region(S) = declared region of S's root if it is a reference binding, else the block region of the root's declaration.

[let x = ConfinedType { fields }] Every region parameter of the type must be instantiated by a captured borrow mint or a moved confined field in this construction; any unbound region parameter rejects (no captureless/forged instantiation). Moved confined field h: rename all entries holder h -> x; merge src(h) into src(x); mark h consumed. Captured borrow of place S with mint mode m: mint legality; precondition and entry addition as in (iv) with kind = kind(ConfinedType); src(x)[the field's region] = S. Kind never changes in any transfer.

[let x = move h] and [rebind x = h] (x previously consumed, identical confined type): rename entries holder h -> x; copy src; h consumed, x live. Assigning over a live confined binding rejects.

[match scrutinee { arms }] If the scrutinee is a live confined binding: require it is an enum whose EVERY variant packs all borrow/confined payload into exactly one confined field of the same kind and region parameters as the scrutinee's type; else reject (struct patterns on confined values always reject). Consume the scrutinee; in each arm, rename its entries to the arm's bound token and copy src. All matches: snapshot state; check each arm from a copy; at arm end run block scope-end processing for arm-locals (below); arms ending in break/return contribute their post-processing state to that exit's join instead. Compare fall-through arm states projected to bindings declared outside the match: identical LoanTable entries (including holder names) and identical liveness, byte-equal, else reject.

[loop { body }] Snapshot entry state; check body once; after body-local scope-end processing, if the back edge is syntactically reachable (not every path ends in break/return — a syntactic test over match-only ANF), require the projected body-end state to equal the entry state exactly, else reject. All break states must be pairwise equal (same projection) and become the post-loop state.

[return e] If e is confined: every region parameter of its type must be a region parameter of the enclosing function, else reject. Run scope-end processing for all locals except e (reverse order). Then exit-effect check: the remaining LoanTable restricted to parameter roots must equal exactly the signature-derived caller effect — entries of consumed own-confined parameters gone, and for each result region 'p exactly (tie('p)-binding, kind, e); any residue or deficit rejects.

[scope end of a block or function] Process the block's bindings in REVERSE declaration order: a live confined binding is auto-consumed — emit its form-declared release (unlock, close, retire) and remove its entries; then, for each owned binding being dropped, if any live entry overlaps its place, REJECT (derived drops obey the freeze); otherwise drop per STOR-3. Reference parameters expire without drops; entries seeded at function entry expire silently at function exit.

[function entry seeding] For each confined parameter h and each region 'p with tie('p) = B (distinct): add (B, kind(h), h) to LoanTable and set src(h)['p] = B. Self-ties seed nothing. Own-mode confined parameters not consumed by the body are auto-consumed at scope end (release runs in the callee — consistent with the caller's derived consume).

[par calls] Per the form's slot marks: replicate-shared slot — argument must be a & mint of a place with no overlapping uniq entry, or a & mint of a live holder all of whose held entries are shr (a holder with any uniq entry rejects); split-unique slot — one &uniq mint, standard mint legality. Body function's effect rows must match slot modes. All mints are consumed by the statement; no entry changes unless an argument is consumed by clause.
repairs_log: [minimal FATAL: gut — untied own-mode token parameter] Adopted strict signature-tie totality (R9): every region parameter of every confined parameter/result must tie to exactly one borrow-mode parameter (self-tie only for by-borrow confined params); gut's 'u is undeclarable, and consume-only helpers must thread the issuer reference — the cost that makes callee seeding total.

[minimal FATAL: launder — strength laundering through a &-tied slot] Loan kind now derives from the confined TYPE's declaration on both sides of every call (R1/R5/R10), never from the tying parameter's mode; a uniq token reissues uniq, so the post-launder t.range rejects at R5. Dropped minimal's mode-derived strength entirely.

[expressive FATAL x2: gut/probe and refresh/relocate — callee bodies checked loan-blind] Adopted the attack's fix verbatim as R10(c): function bodies are checked with entry loan state seeded from the signature (one entry per tied own-confined parameter on its tie binding), plus a return-time exit-effect equality check, so a reissue can only return a token that survived an unmutated source or was freshly issued after the consume.

[expressive-lens FATAL: Two-struct-rings brand collision / Db struct-field undefinedness; minimal risk: brand-through-views] Re-keyed loans and brands from bindings to RESOLVED PLACES (R4), aligned with OWN-5: the brand IS the recorded source place, compared at R10(a); struct-held forms get full token APIs, sibling fields stay independent, and phantom brand parameters (expressive LOAN-7) were deleted outright — loaned places cannot move (R6), so places never go stale.

[minimal MAJOR + expressive-lens MAJOR: TOK-4 rebind falsifies reverse-drop-order] R7 makes compiler-derived drops subject to the freeze: scope end processes bindings in reverse order, auto-consumes live holders, and REJECTS any owned binding dropped while overlapped by a live entry — safety is a checked property, not a positional argument (ATK-drop-order-rebind).

[expressive MAJOR: close_one — LOAN-3(shr)/LOAN-4 carve-out contradiction] The consume-exception is now single and symmetric (R6): permitted only when the consumed holder's entries are the SOLE entries on the source — automatic for uniq, sibling-protecting for shr; this also deliberately ACCEPTS delete-at-sole-cursor, recovering the SQLite shape.

[expressive MAJOR: MaybeTok::None — loanless reissue + brand forgery] R5 bans captureless region instantiation (every region parameter of a user construction must be bound by a captured borrow or moved confined field) and R10(a) requires every confined argument to actually hold the tied loan — both halves of the forgery are closed.

[expressive MAJOR: WView / expressive-lens MINOR: Cloak — kind/field-mode incoherence] R1 coherence: confined(shr) must not transitively contain &uniq or confined(uniq) fields; R8 pins that transfers never change kind.

[expressive MAJOR: memoizing & receiver op races under par] R15 strengthens the trusted obligation: & receiver ops on loanable forms must preserve interior addresses AND be safe under concurrent invocation, under hostile fact-channel review before shipping.

[minimal MAJOR: confined aggregate composition/decomposition undefined; expressive MINOR: EntryResult destructure ban] R3 pins projection as statement-scoped only; R8 pins constructor field moves as holder renames; R11 adds the one sanctioned destructure — enum matches where every variant packs its payload in exactly one same-kind token, renaming per arm — which makes the flagship occupied/vacant entry API writable while struct extraction stays impossible.

[minimal-lens MAJOR: LOAN-8 literal join text rejects frozen ACCEPTs P4/A10] R12 defines the comparison domain once: states are compared as loan entries plus liveness of bindings declared OUTSIDE the construct, after arm/body scope-end processing, holder names included.

[expressive-lens MINOR: back-edge equality demanded on unreachable back edges] R12 conditions the back-edge check on syntactic reachability (every-path-exits is decidable by inspection in match-only ANF).

[minimal-lens MAJOR: try-acquire loops unwritable; expressive-lens MAJOR: fallible acquisition] Three-part answer: R11 covers whole-token fallible enums (wait_timeout style); the state-queryable token (try_lock returns an always-issued Guard with acquired()/dismiss()) is the blessed form-table shape; and acquire-then-exit is writable as a helper whose T arm RETURNS the holder — R10(c)'s exit-effect check plus R13 make the loop-crossing sound (ATK-acquire-helper).

[expressive-lens MAJOR: exactly-one-issuer wrong-rejects multi-source env helpers] R10(b) derives issues per region parameter, one per tied argument place, so mk_env-style multi-borrow helpers compose; only genuinely ambiguous regions (A12 pick) still reject at declaration.

[expressive-lens MAJOR: cursors cannot yield borrowed element views] R5/R15 add the `issues K on source(receiver)` clause: a holder-receiver issue lands as a sibling loan on the holder's recorded source, preserving zero-copy scans for the B-tree/SQLite corpus.

[minimal-lens MINOR: &uniq minting on confined receivers unstated] R3 states it: a live confined binding mints one statement-scoped borrow of either mode per statement, consumed by the receiving call, transferring no entries (P3's c.next()).
[expressive MINOR: plain let-move of a holder loses the release under one completion] R8 covers plain let-moves explicitly: holder renames, release runs at the destination's scope end.
[expressive MINOR: zero-region loanable tokens slip escape] R1 requires >=1 region parameter on loanable confined types; R13 then closes the frame escape.

[minimal MINOR: self-tie ill-foundedness] R9 pins tie resolution order (distinct parameter wins; self-tie only as fallback for by-borrow confined params; anything else rejects) and the machine spec pins that self-tied values seed nothing, skip source comparison, and cannot be re-passed into distinct-tie slots.

[both lenses FATAL/MAJOR: rule-count dishonesty (claimed 12/15, recounted ~26-40)] Restructured to 15 single-topic rules by the trims both attacks proposed: phantom brands deleted (place identity does the work), LOAN-9/10 merged into one join rule (R12), the consume carve-out folded into the freeze rule (R6), clause vocabulary and trusted obligations folded into one form-table rule (R15), rebind folded into holder transfer (R8). Honest statement: each rule is one enforceable judgment area, but an adversarial recount at strictest OWN-granularity would still land near 25-30 — the count is 15 by the same convention OWN-1..13 uses (one numbered normative paragraph per judgment), and the machine spec carries the operational detail so no rule hides a second mechanism.

[minimal MINOR risks retained deliberately] Whole-place uniq loans stay coarse (two disjoint-key entry tokens still reject — A3; escape is a sealed two-key op); arm-balancing consumes remain required (A7/C7) as reject-when-unsure; guards are now uniq-kind (from expressive), so same-thread relock statically rejects instead of deadlocking; the callee-holds-token-during-consuming-call window remains justified by caller suspension and must be re-proved before any async/reentrancy feature; R15's obligations remain the design's largest trusted surface and are explicitly review-gated, not checker-checked.
rules: R1 CONF-1 (Confined formation and kind). A struct or enum type may be declared `confined(uniq)` or `confined(shr)`; it is affine, carries at least one region parameter if it can hold a loan, and its fields may be borrows (&'p T or &uniq 'p T at declared region parameters), confined types instantiated at declared region parameters, or owned non-confined types. Only confined types may contain borrow-typed or confined-typed fields; a non-confined type containing either is ill-formed, and instantiating any generic or sealed-form type parameter with a confined type is ill-formed (checked after monomorphization). A confined(shr) type must not transitively contain any &uniq field or any confined(uniq) field. Sealed-form token, cursor, guard, and endpoint result types are opaque confined types declared in the form table; user code cannot construct opaque confined values.

R2 CONF-2 (Stack confinement). A confined value may exist only as a local let binding, a rebind target, a call argument (by own or by a statement-scoped minted borrow), a call result, a match scrutinee, a match-arm token binding (R11), or a field of another confined value under construction. A statement that would store a confined value into heap data, a sealed-form container, a non-confined aggregate, or a global is rejected at that statement.

R3 CONF-3 (Affinity, minting, projection). A confined binding is affine: passing it by own, destructuring it (R11), and scope end each consume it, and any use after consumption is rejected. A live confined binding may mint one statement-scoped borrow (& or &uniq) per statement, which the receiving call consumes (T-A) and which transfers no loans. Field projection through a live confined binding, or through a borrow of one, yields only a statement-scoped borrow consumed by the receiving call; no multi-statement binding is ever created from a confined value's field except by R11 destructure.

R4 LOAN-1 (Loan state). The checker keeps one loan table per function: a finite set of entries (P, k, h), where P is a resolved place (OWN-5: a root binding plus a possibly empty field path), k in {uniq, shr} is fixed as the declared kind of the holder's confined type, and h is the binding currently holding the token; each holder also records, per region parameter of its type, its source place. A statement touching place Q is governed by every entry whose place overlaps Q (either place a prefix of the other, same root). Entries change only at issue (R5), consume and scope end (R7), and holder transfer (R8, R11); no other analysis reads or writes this state.

R5 LOAN-2 (Issue). An operation or construction issuing a loan of kind k on source place P requires: if k = uniq, no entry overlapping P; if k = shr, no uniq entry overlapping P; and the access to P minted in that statement must itself be legal under R6. The new holder is bound by a fresh let (or rebind), its region parameters instantiate from P (a reference binding's declared region, else the block region of P's root), and (P, k, holder) is added at statement exit. A user confined construction must instantiate every region parameter of its type from a captured borrow mint or a moved confined field in that same construction — leaving any region parameter unbound is rejected — and it issues one entry per captured borrow's source place with the constructed type's kind. An issuing form op whose receiver is itself a live holder issues on that holder's recorded source place (a sibling loan).

R6 LOAN-3 (Freeze). While any entry on P is live, P and every place overlapping it cannot be moved, rebound, assigned over, passed by own, explicitly dropped, or reached by a compiler-derived drop (R7). While a uniq entry (P, uniq, h) is live, no operation on, or borrow mint from, P is permitted; while only shr entries are live on P, & mints, &-receiver operations, and further shr issues on P are permitted, and &uniq mints, &uniq- or own-receiver operations, and uniq issues are rejected. Sole exception in both regimes: a single statement that consumes or reissues holder h may mint the borrows of P that the callee's signature declares, provided h's entries are the only entries on P.

R7 LOAN-4 (Consume and scope end). A call that consumes holder h — declared `consumes`/`reissues` in the form table or derived per R10 — removes every entry held by h after the statement's legality checks and before its issues, so consume-and-reissue composes in one statement. At scope end, bindings are processed in reverse binding order: a live holder is auto-consumed (its compiler-derived drop performs the form-declared release — unlock, close, retire — and removes its entries), and any owned binding still overlapped by a live entry when its derived drop is reached is a checked error. Entries seeded at function entry (R10c) expire at function exit without release.

R8 LOAN-5 (Holder transfer). Moving a live holder — by plain let-move, by `rebind h = h2` (which requires identical confined type and a consumed destination), or into a field of a confined construction — renames its entries' holder to the destination binding (for constructions, to the constructed binding), merges its source records, and consumes the source binding; a loan's kind never changes in transfer, and no operation duplicates a holder or an entry.

R9 SIG-1 (Signature ties). In every function signature, each region parameter 'p of each confined parameter or confined result must tie to exactly one borrow-mode parameter whose declared region is 'p; if no distinct borrow-mode parameter carries 'p and the confined parameter is itself passed by borrow, it ties to itself (self-tie); zero candidates, multiple candidates, or a self-tie needed for an own-mode parameter or a result reject the declaration. Form-table ops declare their loan clause (issues K / issues K on source(receiver) / consumes / reissues / none) in the same vocabulary and are subject to the same call-site checks as derived signatures.

R10 SIG-2 (Signature effects: caller and callee). At every call: (a) each confined argument must hold, for each distinct-tied region parameter, a live entry whose source place is exactly the resolved place of the argument bound to that tie — a missing entry or any mismatch rejects (this is the instance-brand check; self-ties skip it and a self-tied-origin value cannot be passed into a distinct-tie slot); (b) own-mode confined arguments are consumed (R7), and each confined result issues, per region parameter, an entry of the result type's kind on the tied argument's place, held by the freshly bound result, with R5's preconditions checked at the call; (c) every body is checked with its loan table seeded from its own signature — one entry (tie binding, kind, h) per distinct-tied confined parameter h — and at every return, after auto-consuming non-returned locals, the live entries on tied parameters must equal exactly the signature-implied caller effect. The caller trusts only the signature.

R11 MATCH-1 (Confined destructure). A match may destructure a live confined scrutinee only if it is an enum whose every variant packs all of its borrow-typed and confined-typed payload into exactly one confined field of the same kind and region parameters as the scrutinee's type; the match consumes the scrutinee, and in each arm the scrutinee's entries rename to that arm's bound token. Any other destructure of a confined value — any struct pattern, or any variant with zero or several such fields — is rejected; all other field access remains statement-scoped per R3.

R12 JOIN-1 (Match, loop, break, return agreement). Every match arm is checked from a copy of the entry state; states are compared as the loan table and holder liveness restricted to bindings declared outside the construct, taken after the arm's or body's own scope-end processing (R7). All fall-through arms must agree exactly, including holder names; all break states of a loop must agree pairwise and become the post-loop state; a loop body's back-edge state must equal its entry state unless the back edge is syntactically unreachable (every path ends in break or return — decidable by inspection in match-only ANF). Any disagreement rejects; states are never merged by approximation.

R13 ESC-1 (Region escape). A confined value may be returned only if every region parameter of its type is a region parameter of the enclosing function; returning a confined value carrying any locally introduced region is rejected. Because every loanable confined type carries a region parameter (R1), every holder's region is its source's region (R5), and regions are lexical, a holder's scope always lies within its source's scope, and R7's reverse-order scope processing reaches every holder before its source.

R14 PAR-1 (Scoped par boundary). Par bodies are named functions; the par form's signature marks each argument slot replicate-shared or split-unique. A replicate-shared slot accepts a & mint of a source place with no uniq entries, or a & mint of a live holder all of whose held entries are shr; a holder holding any uniq entry is rejected there. A split-unique slot accepts one &uniq mint (legal under R6) that the trusted form divides into disjoint per-body views for the call's extent. All mints are consumed by the par statement, which returns only after joining every body, so every captured region lexically encloses the join; each body's effect row must match its slot modes (no writes through replicated slots; writes only into the body's own split view).

R15 FORM-1 (Fail-closed form table). Every operation of a loanable sealed form carries exactly one loan clause; a call whose loan behavior is neither declared in the form table nor derived by R9/R10 is rejected — absence of declaration is rejection, never default-accept. An op may be declared with & receiver mode on a loanable form only if its internals both preserve every interior address and are safe under concurrent invocation; these obligations, the release actions run by holder drops, and issues-on-source(receiver) clauses are sealed-form proof obligations subject to hostile fact-channel review before shipping — a green checker is not that review.
soundness_sketch: Invariant I: at every program point, for every live confined value V and every region parameter of its type, the loan table holds exactly one entry (P, kind(type(V)), V) where P is the resolved place V was issued from, and every entry's holder is live. Establishment — there are only three creation channels, all forced to record I: form-op and holder-receiver issues (R5), user constructions (R5, whose captureless ban means every region is bound to a captured source — closing ATK-maybetok-none-forgery, the loanless forged-region token), and function-entry seeding (R10c). Kind fidelity: kind is a property of the confined type (R1), never of the access path or tie mode, and coherence forces uniq wherever unique interior access exists — closing ATK-launder-strength (a uniq token can never be reborn shr through a &-tied slot) and ATK-wview/ATK-cloak (no shr wrapper carries unique interior paths, so the optimizer's shr-window fact holds). Preservation: R1/R2 make every untracked position unrepresentable (heap, non-confined aggregates, generic and sealed-form instantiation post-monomorphization — A2/C2); transfers rename and never duplicate (R8 — closing ATK-let-move-holder's lost-release completion); destructure renames under the every-variant-one-token condition (R11 — closing ATK-destructure-env-struct, since struct extraction is banned and projection is statement-scoped per R3); joins reject any bookkeeping disagreement (R12). Exclusion given I: any statement that could mutate, move, reallocate, or drop a source overlaps a loaned place and rejects under R6 — moves and compiler-derived drops included, so ATK-drop-order-rebind rejects at the drop itself (R7) rather than by a positional argument the rebind falsified; place keying extends the freeze to struct-held forms (ATK-db-struct-field) while leaving sibling fields free, and place identity is the brand — ATK-two-struct-rings and C13-relock fail the R10(a) place comparison, and places cannot go stale because loaned roots cannot move (R6). Helper boundary — where both drafts broke: R9 totality means no confined parameter arrives untied (ATK-gut undeclarable); R10(c) seeding reconstructs the caller's loan inside the callee frame, so ATK-probe, ATK-refresh, and ATK-relocate all reject in the body; R10(a) verifies the loan's existence and place at every call; the R6 sole-loan condition confines the consume-exception to statements surrendering the only token (ATK-close-one-sibling rejects; ATK-delete-at-sole is statement-atomic with no sibling to dangle); and the return-time exit-effect equality (R10c) means a derived reissue can only return a token that survived an unmutated-source body or was freshly issued after the consume — the laundering channel is gone. The one untracked window — the callee holds interior access during a consuming call — is safe because the caller is suspended for the call's whole extent, the callee is checked under the same judgment with seeded state, and single-threaded program order makes the statement atomic; this justification must be re-proved before any async, signal, or reentrant-callback feature. Across the scoped-par boundary: R14 admits only shr-backed replication (any uniq-holding token rejects — C14) and form-guaranteed disjoint split views, the par statement consumes all mints and joins every body before returning (so every captured region encloses the join), body rows are checked syntactically, and R15's strengthened obligation — address stability AND concurrent-invocation safety for & receiver ops — closes ATK-memoizing-shared-get at the declaration gate. Zero-region escapes close at R1 plus R13 (ATK-zero-region-endpoint), so every holder dies before its source with R7's reverse-order processing a checked property. Hence in any accepted program, every mutation, move, reallocation, or drop of a source is ordered strictly after the consume of every token carrying interior access into it: no token can dangle, single-threaded or across par.
